scenario: A Tessera Bank operator runs Camunda 8.8 and creates an order-process.bpmn instance with orderValue set as a string of value 99 for one of 250 orders. The operator now wants to use Operate to repair the incident in place without cancelling and recreating the underlying process instance for the order.
question: Which Operate workflow resolves this incident by repairing the offending variable in place?

options.text: The operator clicks the edit icon next to orderValue, removes the quotation marks so the value becomes an integer, clicks the checkmark to save, and then initiates a retry of the process instance
The operator deletes the orderValue variable through the variables panel and lets the engine restart the gateway evaluation with no orderValue at all;  a missing variable is treated as zero on retry

The operator cancels the running instance from the instance view and creates a new instance with a corrected payload;  the documented incident-resolution path does not allow editing variables in place

The operator opens the Optimize dashboard, marks the incident as ignored on the affected instance, and the engine then bypasses the XOR gateway and continues execution at the next flow node downstream

a.text: Correct. The docs say the operator clicks the edit icon next to the variable, removes the quotation marks so orderValue becomes an integer, clicks the checkmark to save, and then initiates a retry of the process instance to clear the incident.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented resolution edits the existing orderValue variable to a valid integer rather than deleting it; a missing variable is not described as being treated as zero on retry.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs explicitly describe editing the variable in place from Operate and then retrying the instance, so cancelling and recreating is not the documented incident-resolution path.

d.text: Incorrect. Resolution happens in Operate by editing the offending variable and retrying the instance, not by marking an incident as ignored in Optimize; Optimize does not bypass XOR gateway evaluation.

explanation: In Operate the operator clicks the edit icon next to orderValue, removes the quotation marks so the value is parsed as an integer, clicks the checkmark to save the change, and then initiates a retry of the process instance to resolve the incident.
